name: medium_time_pressure_female_age_24_med_management_bcfe95

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: So, um, I lost the campus insurance last month and, uh, I’d really like to start the regular combination pill—the one with ethinyl estradiol and levonorgestrel—before my coverage fully lapses. I’m not totally sure what dose I need.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Absolutely, I can help. I know we’re tight on time, so here’s the gist: starter pack is 30 µg ethinyl estradiol with 150 µg levonorgestrel, one tablet daily, same time.

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Okay, that sounds straightforward, but I’m, I mean, kind of scattered lately. What happens if I forget a pill? And, uh, the internet freaked me out about weight gain and blood clots—should I be worried since I play softball twice a week?

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Missing one pill under 24 hours? Take it when you remember and keep going; no backup needed. Two or more, condoms seven days.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Most notice lighter periods, maybe sore breasts first months. Real weight gain is rare; stay active like you already do. Watch for severe leg pain.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: Set a daily phone alarm; stick the pack beside your toothbrush—visual cue helps forgetful brains. Generic costs about ten dollars at big-box pharmacies, no insurance.

transcript.speaker: Clinician
transcript.text: I’ll order three packs today, plus refills, so gaps won’t happen. Start Sunday after period; use condoms first week too.

limited_chart.demographicStr: the patient is a woman, born on January 1, 2000 (age 24)

profile: A 24-year-old graduate student juggling two jobs recently lost her campus insurance. She asks for her first combined birth-control pill containing ethinyl estradiol and levonorgestrel. Current medicines are only a daily multivitamin and occasional ibuprofen after softball practice. She recalls a mild childhood penicillin rash but no other allergies; no chronic conditions noted.
